definition: Every modern default cycle (2001, 2008, 2015, 2020) included counter-cyclical labor market loosening as margin relief. At 0.3% labor force growth this valve is structurally impaired, and 87% covenant-lite with 25-30% EBITDA addbacks calibrated to higher labor growth compound the miscalibration. Direction is near-certain but magnitude is unknowable.
evidence: 4/4 modern default cycles featured labor market loosening as margin relief
0.3% labor force growth structurally impairs the labor loosening valve
25-30% EBITDA addbacks embed labor synergies calibrated to 0.8-1.2% labor force growth
N=0 for the combination of these conditions
2-3pp default uplift is structured hypothesis, not calibrated estimate
open_questions: What is the actual magnitude of default uplift?
When will the thesis become testable — unfalsifiability critique is methodologically valid?
Can firms adapt through non-labor margin relief channels?
